pass_criterion: A strict 5-lens council review scores the skill output at mean >= 8.5/10 with no lens below 8.0, and the output produces the named work product, holds the human stop-line, covers adverse/negative paths, and writes context back into a retrievable artifact. For research cases, the output must produce a Research Finding Dossier, keep claims separate from evidence, preserve the human credibility or worth-knowing verdict, and write the ledger/graph/blind-spot context back for future retrieval.

evals.name: vet-batch-of-ai-generated-claims
evals.prompt: I had an agent generate ~40 candidate claims about whether intermittent fasting extends lifespan, pulled from across the literature. Some look solid, some look fringe, and a couple are wild. I don't have time to read every source. Which of these should I actually believe, and how do I hand back a defensible answer instead of just a vibe?
evals.expected_output: A Research Finding Dossier: the finding, a two-axis credibility ledger (Ⅰ–Ⅴ evidence grade kept separate from paradigm distance, claims unmixed from evidence), a knowledge-graph contribution, and a blind-spot register; the weak×far claims suspended for targeted evidence-seeking rather than deleted; the final credibility verdict reserved to the human.
assertions: Produces a credibility ledger that grades evidence Ⅰ–Ⅴ and keeps claims unmixed from evidence (separate columns).
Uses two axes (evidence strength AND distance-from-paradigm), never a single collapsed credibility score.
Applies the four-quadrant disposition and explicitly SUSPENDS weak-evidence × far-from-paradigm claims for targeted evidence-seeking rather than deleting them.
Triages: hands the bulk traversal/scoring to agents but reserves the FINAL credibility verdict (vouch / vouch-with-caveats / withhold) to a named human.
Records a knowledge-graph contribution (nodes, evidence edges, any conflict edges) and a blind-spot register.
Treats the dossier, ledger, graph, and register as the retrievable work product rather than advice.
Does not collapse into 'read faster' — it redraws the loop around replication + the ledger as a load-bearing verifier.

evals.name: literature-synthesis-worth-knowing
evals.prompt: 帮我做一个关于「大语言模型是否真的会推理，还是只是统计模式匹配」的文献综述。市面上观点很乱，我想要一个能站得住脚的结论，而不是把几十篇论文摘要拼在一起。
evals.expected_output: A Research Finding Dossier centered on integration (not retrieval): a synthesized finding with a credibility ledger that grades anchors Ⅰ–Ⅴ and caveats observational/model-predicted/self-reported claims, a knowledge-graph contribution with conflict edges where papers contradict, a blind-spot register, and the human-held worth-knowing + credibility verdicts.
assertions: Distinguishes integration (the scarce act — stitching claims never before placed together) from retrieval (abundified), and treats the deliverable as a synthesis, not a stack of summaries.
Grades load-bearing citations Ⅰ–Ⅴ and labels register (observational ≠ causal, model-predicted, self-reported) rather than asserting them as fact.
Surfaces contradictions between sources as explicit conflict edges in the knowledge-graph contribution (the falsifiable property), not buried.
Reserves the final credibility verdict and the worth-knowing call to the human; states which truth is worth knowing for whom under which frame where relevant.
Treats weak or conflicting evidence as an adverse path to preserve and investigate, not smooth over.
Includes a blind-spot register naming scope edges and what to re-check, plus a future-trajectory line.

evals.name: is-this-direction-worth-pursuing
evals.prompt: I'm an academic deciding what to work on next. An agent gave me 12 'high-impact' research directions in my subfield, all of which have datasets and benchmarks ready. They all feel safe and a bit boring. Help me figure out which of these is actually worth pursuing — or whether I'm being funneled into the safe in-paradigm zone.
evals.expected_output: A worth-knowing analysis: names the generation layer's conservative/homogenization bias and the funnel into the in-paradigm zone, separates community-mean taste (learnable, what the agent surfaced) from off-mean frontier value, keeps the worth-knowing call as the constitutive human node, and (since this borders direction/governance) flags the hand-off; produces a dossier framing rather than auto-picking a direction.
assertions: Names that the candidate set is likely already narrowed by the generation layer's conservative bias (benchmarked, data-rich, in-paradigm) and that saved capacity does not auto-become exploration slack.
Distinguishes learnable community-mean taste from the off-mean frontier value, and treats 'worth knowing' as a constitutive judgment (worth for whom, under which value frame), not a capability/score.
Does NOT auto-select a single direction for the user; keeps the worth-knowing call as the reserved human judgment node and asks/frames the value question.
Requires the candidate set to include paradigm-level options rather than only the supplied in-paradigm twelve.
Writes the dossier framing and boundary hand-off as retrievable context for future direction review.
Flags the boundary/hand-off to direction-ownership (governance) and respects it instead of silently automating it.
